models.label: Llama 3.3 70B (Workers AI)
models.note: Cloudflare Workers AI — the included inference backbone (no BYOK key). Strong, fast 70B tool-caller served on Cloudflare's edge; the recommended default for most swarms. Billed to the platform Cloudflare account.

models.label: Llama 3.1 8B (Workers AI)
models.note: Cloudflare Workers AI — cheapest/fastest included tier (no key). Good for simple single-tool minds; prefer the 70B for complex multi-tool swarms.

models.label: Qwen2.5-Coder 32B (Workers AI)
models.note: Cloudflare Workers AI — strong coder/tool-caller (no key). Best Workers-AI pick for code-heavy or structured-output minds.

models.label: Claude Opus 4.8
models.note: Most capable Opus-tier model; state-of-the-art long-horizon agentic execution and tool use. Best default for demanding tool-using minds. 1M context, 128K max output. Adaptive thinking only (no budget_tokens); assistant prefill removed. $5/$25 per MTok.

models.label: Claude Fable 5
models.note: Anthropic's most capable widely released model for the hardest reasoning/long-horizon agentic work. Above Opus pricing ($10/$50 per MTok). Thinking always on (omit thinking param); safety classifiers may return stop_reason 'refusal' (enable fallbacks); requires 30-day data retention (no ZDR). Not the default Opus upgrade — select explicitly.

models.label: Claude Opus 4.7
models.note: Previous-generation Opus; highly autonomous, very strong on long-horizon agentic/tool-use work. Same API surface as 4.8. 1M context, 128K max output. $5/$25 per MTok.

models.label: Claude Sonnet 4.6
models.note: Best speed/intelligence balance; reliable tool use, recommended for high-volume production swarms. Adaptive thinking. 1M context, 64K max output. $3/$15 per MTok.

models.label: Claude Haiku 4.5
models.note: Fastest and cheapest current model; tool use is reliable. Good for speed-critical or lightweight agents. 200K context, 64K max output. $1/$5 per MTok.

models.label: Claude Opus 4.6
models.note: Older Opus, still active. Adaptive thinking (recommended); budget_tokens deprecated. 1M context, 128K max output. Use 4.8 for new work unless pinning.

models.label: Claude Opus 4.5
models.note: Legacy but still active. Supports adaptive thinking and the effort parameter. Offer only if a user needs to pin this version; prefer Opus 4.8.

models.label: Claude Sonnet 4.5
models.note: Legacy Sonnet, still active. No effort parameter; reliable tool use. Prefer Sonnet 4.6 for new minds; offer for version pinning only.

models.label: GPT-5
models.note: Current flagship; best OpenAI choice for agentic/tool-using minds (strong function calling, parallel tools, instruction following). Reasoning model: on Chat Completions use max_completion_tokens (NOT max_tokens); Responses API uses max_output_tokens. Requires an API key (BYOK).

models.label: GPT-4.1
models.note: Non-reasoning workhorse, 1M context, explicitly built for agents (big gains in instruction following + tool calling over 4o). Accepts the classic max_tokens parameter. Solid, predictable choice for tool-using minds. Needs API key.

models.label: GPT-4.1 mini
models.note: Cheap, fast, 1M context; OpenAI highlights it as strong at instruction following and tool calling. Excellent low-cost default for agentic minds. Uses max_tokens.

models.label: GPT-5.4 mini
models.note: Newer GPT-5.4 refresh of the mini tier (text+image, function calling, web/file search, computer use). Per OpenAI docs ~$0.75/$4.50 per 1M in/out. Reasoning model: use max_completion_tokens (NOT max_tokens). Confirm availability on your account.

models.label: GPT-5 mini
models.note: Cheaper/faster GPT-5 tier; succeeds o4-mini. Good default for cost-sensitive tool-using minds. Reasoning model: use max_completion_tokens on Chat Completions (NOT max_tokens). Needs API key.

models.label: o4-mini (legacy reasoning)
models.note: Fast cost-efficient reasoning model; now marked deprecated in OpenAI docs, succeeded by gpt-5-mini. Still callable on many accounts. Reasoning model: requires max_completion_tokens (NOT max_tokens). Prefer gpt-5-mini for new minds.

models.label: GPT-5.4 nano
models.note: Newest smallest/cheapest tier; recommended for classification, data extraction, ranking, coding subagents rather than full autonomous minds. Use max_completion_tokens (NOT max_tokens). Confirm availability on your account.

models.label: GPT-5 nano
models.note: Smallest/cheapest GPT-5; aimed at classification, extraction, ranking, coding subagents. Tool calling works but the small tier is less reliable for complex multi-tool swarms — validate before trusting in a mind. Use max_completion_tokens (NOT max_tokens).

models.label: GPT-4.1 nano
models.note: Cheapest 4.1 tier; fast 1M-context model for light tasks. Function calling supported but the nano tier is weaker for complex multi-tool orchestration — verify per swarm. Uses max_tokens.

models.label: Qwen2.5-Coder 7B
models.note: Best value free-local tool caller for agentic/coding minds. ~4.7GB download, runs in ~6-8GB VRAM/RAM at Q4. Solid, consistent function-calling for single and small multi-tool loops. Default recommendation for most users.

models.label: Qwen2.5-Coder 14B
models.note: Step up from 7B; noticeably better at multi-step tool plans and arg formatting. ~9GB download, wants ~12-16GB VRAM/RAM at Q4. Good sweet spot for serious agentic work on a 16GB machine.

models.label: Qwen2.5 7B Instruct
models.note: General-purpose sibling of the coder; reliable native tool support, better for non-code conversational minds. ~4.7GB, ~6-8GB VRAM/RAM at Q4.

models.label: Qwen2.5 14B Instruct
models.note: More capable general tool-using mind; handles multi-tool reasoning better than 7B. ~9GB, ~12-16GB VRAM/RAM at Q4.

models.label: Mistral Nemo 12B
models.note: Native tool support, 128K context (big win for long agent transcripts), multilingual. ~7.1GB, fits 12GB VRAM at Q4_K_M. Strong 'step up from 8B' pick for quality-sensitive minds.

models.label: Qwen2.5-Coder 32B
models.note: Strongest free-local coder/tool caller in this family; most robust in complex swarms. ~20GB download, needs ~24GB+ VRAM (or 32GB+ RAM, slow on CPU). 'cost=high' = local hardware burden, not a fee.

models.label: Mistral Small 3 24B
models.note: Owns the agentic/structured-JSON niche with strong native function calling; very steady in multi-tool loops. ~14GB download, wants ~24GB VRAM (or 32GB RAM). 'cost=high' = hardware, not a fee.

models.label: Command R 35B
models.note: Cohere model purpose-built for RAG + tool use; very reliable structured tool calls and citations. Heavy: ~20GB download, ~24GB+ VRAM. 'cost=high' = local hardware burden, not a fee.

models.label: Qwen3 14B
models.note: Stronger Qwen3 with tool+thinking support and better agent reasoning than 8B. Caveat: confirm thinking-mode tool calls are stable on your Ollama build. ~9.3GB, ~12-16GB VRAM at Q4.

models.label: Qwen3 8B
models.note: Tools + thinking modes; agent-oriented. HONEST: tool-calling in thinking mode can be inconsistent across Ollama versions — verify against your swarm before trusting. ~5.2GB, ~8GB VRAM at Q4.

models.label: Llama 3.1 8B
models.note: HONEST: emits tool calls but is unreliable in complex multi-tool swarms (drops/duplicates calls, malformed args). Fine for simple single-tool minds. ~4.7GB, runs in ~6GB VRAM. Prefer qwen2.5-coder:7b for real agentic use.

models.label: Llama 3.2 3B
models.note: Lightweight (runs on ~4GB, even CPU-tolerable). Supports tools but small size means shaky arg accuracy and weak multi-step planning. Good for cheap/edge minds with one or two simple tools.

models.label: Kimi K2 (0905) on Groq
models.note: Moonshot Kimi K2, 1T MoE / 32B active, purpose-built for agentic tool use; tops tool-use benchmarks. Groq serves it at very high tok/s. Excellent default for minds. Groq id exactly as shown.

models.label: GPT-OSS 120B on Groq
models.note: OpenAI open-weight reasoning model, strong agentic tool use, native function calling. Groq id is namespaced 'openai/gpt-oss-120b'. Good agentic pick on Groq's fast inference.

models.label: Qwen3 32B on Groq
models.note: Qwen3 32B, strong instruction-following + agent tool use, multilingual. Groq id namespaced 'qwen/qwen3-32b'. Good mid-size agentic option.

models.label: Llama 3.3 70B Versatile on Groq
models.note: Meta Llama 3.3 70B, solid + cheap tool-caller, parallel tool use supported on Groq. The 70B handles complex swarms far better than llama3.1:8b (which tool-calls poorly).

models.label: Qwen3 235B A22B Instruct (2507) on Together
models.note: Qwen3 235B MoE (22B active), instruction-tuned, optimized for reasoning/code/tool usage, ~262K ctx. Together id is HF-style 'Qwen/...'. Strong agentic flagship-tier open model.

models.label: Llama 3.3 70B Instruct Turbo on Together
models.note: Meta Llama 3.3 70B Turbo on Together, function calling supported. Reliable, low-cost agentic baseline. A -Free variant (meta-llama/Llama-3.3-70B-Instruct-Turbo-Free) exists for testing.

models.label: DeepSeek V3.2 on Together
models.note: DeepSeek V3.2 (sparse-attention MoE) tuned for reasoning + agentic tool use. Together id HF-style 'deepseek-ai/...'. DeepSeek tool-calling is good but historically less rock-solid than Qwen/Kimi in dense swarms — verify per-mind. Confirm exact casing against Together's live catalog.

models.label: Kimi K2 on OpenRouter
models.note: Kimi K2 via OpenRouter (routes to cheapest/fastest upstream). Top-tier agentic tool use. OpenRouter slug 'moonshotai/kimi-k2'. Good when you want provider failover behind one key.

models.label: Qwen3 235B A22B on OpenRouter
models.note: Qwen3 235B-A22B via OpenRouter, optimized for tool usage, native ~262K ctx. Slug 'qwen/qwen3-235b-a22b'. Append ':free' for the zero-cost rate-limited tier if available. Strong agentic open model.

models.label: DeepSeek Chat V3.1 on OpenRouter
models.note: DeepSeek V3.1 chat via OpenRouter, supports structured tool calling (V3.1 Terminus variant: deepseek/deepseek-v3.1-terminus). Capable + cheap; DeepSeek tool reliability in complex swarms is good-not-perfect — test per-mind.

models.label: Free (rotating free tier) on OpenRouter
models.note: OpenRouter's own ROTATING free-tier router: 'openrouter/free' selects a currently-free model at random on every request (their free pool is grant/web3-subsidized and churns, so this never goes stale). $0 tokens — still needs an OpenRouter key, and free models may require enabling free-model/training consent in OpenRouter privacy settings. Which underlying model you get varies per call, so tool-calling reliability varies — validate for anything demanding; ideal for cheap/experimental minds.

models.label: Llama 3.3 70B (free) on OpenRouter
models.note: Meta Llama 3.3 70B via OpenRouter's free tier ($0). Reliable tool caller for a free model. The ':free' suffix is OpenRouter's free-variant convention; this specific free listing can rotate out over time — use openrouter/free for a self-maintaining rotator.

models.label: Qwen3 Coder (free) on OpenRouter
models.note: Qwen3 Coder via OpenRouter free tier ($0), strong for code + tool use. ':free' free-variant listing; may rotate out over time — openrouter/free is the durable rotating option.

models.label: GPT-OSS 20B (free) on OpenRouter
models.note: OpenAI open-weight GPT-OSS 20B via OpenRouter free tier ($0). Good cheap agentic option; small tier so validate tool reliability in complex swarms. ':free' listing may rotate out — prefer openrouter/free for a self-maintaining rotator.

models.label: Auto Router (best model) on OpenRouter
models.note: OpenRouter's meta Auto Router: routes your prompt to whichever of dozens of models gives the best output (variable pricing, billed at the chosen model's rate — NOT free). The response's returned model id tells you which was used. Good 'just pick something good' default when you don't want to choose.

models.label: Kimi K2 (0905) on Fireworks
models.note: Kimi K2 on Fireworks, function calling supported. Fireworks ids use 'accounts/fireworks/models/...' path form. Strong agentic default with Fireworks' fast serverless inference.

models.label: DeepSeek V3.1 on Fireworks
models.note: DeepSeek V3.1 on Fireworks (note Fireworks spells the version 'v3p1', dot->p). Function calling supported. Newer deepseek-v3p2 also available. DeepSeek tool-calling: verify in dense swarms.

models.label: Mistral Medium 3.5
models.note: Dated id mistral-medium-3-5. Frontier-class, explicitly tuned for agentic + coding; best-in-class reliable multi-tool calling and long-horizon tasks. Configurable reasoning_effort per request. Best price/perf agentic pick here.

models.label: Mistral Large 3 (2512)
models.note: Flagship open-weight multimodal model. Native function calling, parallel tool calls, structured outputs, Agents API. Dated id: mistral-large-2512. Strong agentic default.

models.label: Devstral 2
models.note: Frontier code-agent model for software-engineering tasks (SWE-bench class). Tool use central to its design. Use for code-heavy minds; less general-purpose than Medium 3.5.

models.label: Mistral Small 4 (2603)
models.note: Dated id mistral-small-2603. Hybrid instruct+reasoning+coding multimodal, tool calling + extended reasoning. Cheap workhorse for swarms; good agentic value. Also open-weight (Apache-2.0) for local hosting.

models.label: Ministral 3 8B
models.note: Edge/latency-tier (also 3B and 14B variants: ministral-3-3b-2512 / ministral-3-14b-2512). Supports tools but small models tool-call less reliably in complex swarms — verify per deployment. Open-weight, runnable locally.

models.label: DeepSeek V4 Flash
models.note: Current DeepSeek default (284B/13B-active MoE). 1M context, tool use + function calling, agentic-coding SOTA among open models. OpenAI-compatible base_url https://api.deepseek.com (or /v1). Cheap, strong tool caller. Replaces deepseek-chat non-thinking mode.

models.label: DeepSeek V4 Pro
models.note: Larger MoE (1.6T/49B-active). 1M context, tool use + function calling; DeepSeek's own pick for complex long-horizon agentic coding. Use when V4-Flash falls short on multi-step tool chains.

models.label: DeepSeek Chat (legacy alias)
models.note: Legacy alias = non-thinking mode of deepseek-v4-flash. Function calling supported. DEPRECATES 2026-07-24 — migrate to deepseek-v4-flash. Listed only for back-compat; do not pick for new minds.

models.label: Gemini 3.5 Flash
models.note: Current most-intelligent Gemini for sustained agentic + coding. 1M context, function calling via tools/tool_choice. OpenAI endpoint base_url https://generativelanguage.googleapis.com/v1beta/openai/ , model field = bare id 'gemini-3.5-flash'. Top agentic pick for Google.

models.label: Gemini 3.1 Pro (preview)
models.note: Highest-capability Gemini, advanced reasoning + powerful agentic/coding. 1M context, function calling. Preview channel — id may change; confirm before pinning. Use for hardest multi-step tool reasoning.

models.label: Gemini 2.5 Flash
models.note: Stable price/perf reasoning model, function calling + web search + structured output. 1M context. Cheaper, fully-GA alternative to 3.5 Flash for high-volume swarms. Budget tiers: gemini-2.5-flash-lite (cheapest 2.5).

models.label: GPT-OSS 120B (HF Inference Providers)
models.note: OpenAI open-weight reasoning model routed through Hugging Face's provider network (Cerebras/Groq/Together/...). One hf_ fine-grained token (Inference Providers permission), OpenAI-compatible at https://router.huggingface.co/v1. Default routes to the fastest provider; append ':provider' (e.g. ':groq') or ':cheapest' to the model id to pin one. Strong agentic tool caller.

models.label: DeepSeek V3 (HF Inference Providers)
models.note: DeepSeek V3 via HF Inference Providers (one hf_ token, auto provider routing). OpenAI-compatible router endpoint. DeepSeek tool-calling is good but verify in dense swarms. Append ':provider'/':cheapest' to pin routing.

models.label: Llama 3.3 70B (HF Inference Providers)
models.note: Meta Llama 3.3 70B via HF Inference Providers. Reliable, low-cost agentic baseline behind one hf_ token; the router auto-selects the fastest upstream provider. Append ':provider'/':cheapest' to pin.

models.label: Qwen2.5 Coder 32B (HF Inference Providers)
models.note: Strong coder/tool-caller via HF Inference Providers (one hf_ token). Best HF pick for code-heavy or structured-output minds. Append ':provider'/':cheapest' to pin routing.

models.label: GLM-5.2
models.note: Z.AI (Zhipu) flagship GLM-5.2. OpenAI-compatible chat/completions at https://api.z.ai/api/paas/v4 with Bearer auth; model field = bare id 'glm-5.2'. Strong agentic tool use + coding. Newest 5.x flagship — the top Z.AI pick for demanding minds.

models.label: GLM-4.6
models.note: GLM-4.6, ~200K context, ~30% more token-efficient than 4.5. Reliable native function calling; excellent agentic/coding value on Z.AI. Solid default before reaching for 5.2.

models.label: GLM-4.5
models.note: GLM-4.5 (355B MoE / 32B active), agent-oriented with strong tool use + reasoning. ~128K context. Dependable mid-tier agentic model; prefer 4.6/5.2 for new minds.

models.label: GLM-4.5 Air
models.note: GLM-4.5 Air (106B MoE / 12B active) — lighter, faster, cheaper sibling of 4.5 tuned for agentic tool use. Good high-volume/low-latency pick.

models.label: GLM-4.5 Flash (free)
models.note: GLM-4.5 Flash — Z.AI's free tier. Fast and no-cost; supports tools but the small/free tier is less reliable in complex multi-tool swarms — validate per-mind. Great for testing the Z.AI provider without spend.

models.label: GLM 5.2 (TokenGO)
models.note: Z.AI GLM-5.2 flagship served via TokenGO (self-operated inference at a discount; official API as fallback). OpenAI-compatible at https://api.thorbase.com/v1, Bearer auth, model field = vendor slug 'z-ai/glm-5.2'. 1M context. ~$1.26/$3.96 per 1M in/out. Top TokenGO pick for demanding agentic minds.

models.label: Kimi K2.6 (TokenGO)
models.note: Moonshot Kimi K2.6 (text+vision) via TokenGO. Purpose-built for agentic tool use; tops tool-use benchmarks. 262K context. ~$0.61/$3.07 per 1M in/out. Slug 'moonshotai/kimi-k2.6'. Excellent agentic default.

models.label: DeepSeek V4 Pro (TokenGO)
models.note: DeepSeek V4 Pro (large MoE) via TokenGO, 1M context, strong long-horizon agentic coding. ~$0.43/$0.87 per 1M in/out — very cheap for its tier. Slug 'deepseek/deepseek-v4-pro'.

models.label: DeepSeek V4 Flash (TokenGO)
models.note: DeepSeek V4 Flash via TokenGO, 1M context, reliable tool caller. Cheapest strong option here: ~$0.10/$0.20 per 1M in/out. Slug 'deepseek/deepseek-v4-flash'. Great high-volume default.

models.label: Qwen3.5 397B A17B (TokenGO)
models.note: Qwen3.5 397B-A17B MoE (text+vision) via TokenGO, strong instruction-following + agent tool use. 262K context. ~$0.40/$2.65 per 1M in/out. Slug 'qwen/qwen3.5-397b-a17b'.

models.label: MiniMax M2.5 (TokenGO)
models.note: MiniMax M2.5 via TokenGO, 197K context. Cheap (~$0.14/$0.81 per 1M in/out); verify tool-calling reliability in complex swarms. Slug 'minimax/minimax-m2.5'.

models.label: GLM 5.1 (TokenGO)
models.note: Z.AI GLM-5.1 via TokenGO, 203K context, agent-tuned. ~$0.88/$2.80 per 1M in/out. Slug 'z-ai/glm-5.1'. Cheaper step below 5.2.

models.label: GLM 5 (TokenGO)
models.note: Z.AI GLM-5 via TokenGO, 203K context. ~$0.48/$1.54 per 1M in/out — cheapest of the GLM-5 line here. Slug 'z-ai/glm-5'.

models.label: DeepSeek V3.2 (TokenGO)
models.note: DeepSeek V3.2 (sparse-attention MoE) via TokenGO, 131K context. Very cheap (~$0.22/$0.33). DeepSeek tool-calling is good but verify in dense swarms. Slug 'deepseek/deepseek-v3.2'.

models.label: DeepSeek V3.1 (TokenGO)
models.note: DeepSeek V3.1 via TokenGO, 164K context. ~$0.19/$0.71 per 1M in/out. Structured tool calling supported; verify per-mind. Slug 'deepseek/deepseek-v3.1'.
